cards.notes: Starter strike, replace when possible
Starter defend, replace when possible
Starter. 2-cost is clunky but vulnerable application is useful
Rare power, slow draw/scaling engine. Not needed when infinite is so consistent
0-cost attack, strong act 1 pick. Pairs with Burning Pact as free exhaust fodder
1-cost block + upgrade. Essentially just a defend, too slow in current meta
1-cost slow scaling damage. If you're exhausting enough for this to matter, better payoffs exist
3-cost power, block retention. Much worse than Spire 1 — no enablers for block builds
0-cost draw 3. Lines up combo pieces, great for finding exhaust + energy cards
2-cost ~14 block. Not needed when going infinite, doesn't help win fights faster
0-cost reusable energy at COMMON. Core infinite enabler. Lose 3 HP often a bonus. Pick on sight
3-cost 32 damage, uncommon. Big bonk for act 1, combos with Bloodletting energy. Skip once engine assembled
1-cost damage = block. No enablers for block builds in Spire 2, Feel No Pain much worse, no Power Through
0-cost targeted exhaust + 1 str, lose 1 HP. Rare hurts it but zero cost exhaust is incredible
Ancient boon. 2-cost 20 damage + 5 vuln. Not needed when infinite by act 2, worse than other boon options
1-cost AoE 9 damage, lose 1 HP. Worst AoE but AoE is AoE. Act 1 survival card
0-cost attack. Energy plays more often on Clad due to Bash. Bottom of B, vuln synergy mostly irrelevant
1-cost exhaust + draw 2 (3 upgraded). #1 infinite enabler. Self-cycles, does both exhaust and draw. Pick on sight
X-cost play top X cards. Awkward, can play wrong cards at wrong time. Doesn't enable infinite
2-cost 17 damage, exhaust top of draw pile. No control over what gets exhausted, can brick your run
1-cost block + 50% less vuln damage. Common but not needed when going infinite. Bottom of B
1-cost rare AoE. Bonus damage per attack played but outclassed by uncommon Stomp. Rarely enters deck
Ancient boon. Skills cost 0 and exhaust. Dark Embrace now rare, Power Through gone. Much worse context
1-cost rare power, lose 1 HP gain 8 block/turn. Doesn't block turn played. Awkward, never justifiable late
1-cost power, vuln enemies take 25% more. Crazy effect but irrelevant when playing infinite attacks
2-cost rare power, draw 1 on exhaust. Upgrades to 1-cost. Insane acceleration, draws entire deck
3-cost +2 str/turn. Curse in endgame when infinite. Only useful to kill act 1 boss/elites
0-cost block with exhaust. Multiplayer focused but exhausts for free
1-cost 8 damage, hits twice if vuln. Effectively 24 damage with Bash vuln. Solid act 1 card
1-cost gain str per vuln on enemy, exhaust. Draw order reliant, you'd rather just play a strike
0-cost draw 2 then exhaust top each turn. Can brick infinite by exhausting key cards. Extremely niche
1-cost 8 block, 16 if exhausted this turn. Lines up with Burning Pact. Can stall to exhaust down
2-cost gain energy per attack in hand. Makes 5-7 energy/turn. Reusable. Disgusting energy generation
1-cost +3 max HP on kill, exhaust. Great long-run value but doesn't enable engine. Less needed in Spire 2
1-cost +3 block per exhaust. Biggest fall from grace — was easy S in Spire 1. Not needed when going infinite
2-cost exhaust hand, deal 7 per card. Mass exhaust, often wins fight next turn. Dark Embrace now rare though
2-cost deal 5x2, gain 2 str, enemy gains 1 str. Buffs enemy. Makes run harder. Never pick
2-cost 12 block + 4 thorns. Good early game, great vs multi-attack enemies. Top of B
1-cost gain 3 energy if exhausted this turn. Upgrades to 4 (net +3). Infinite energy generation. Broken
1-cost deal 7 + damage per block gained this turn. Numbers too small, you'd rather play Bash + attacks
1-cost play + exhaust top of draw pile. All enablers from Spire 1 gone (Frozen Eye, more Dark Embrace)
1-cost damage + top deck from discard. Puts Bash/combos on top. Decent act 1 but doesn't contribute to infinite
2-cost rare power, strikes auto-play. Fun but soft-lock protection limits it. Doesn't help infinite plan
1-cost lose 2 HP deal 14. Big early damage, combos with Bash vuln. Not exceptional like Spire 1
3-cost AoE 16, plays from exhaust pile. Awkward — needs manual exhaust, returns to deck. Bottom of C
2-cost 30 block, exhaust. Slam on sight in act 1, huge number. Don't pick once infinite is assembled
1-cost random attack to hand, exhaust. Too random, less useful attacks to cheat out. Desperation only
Power: lose 1 HP/turn, deal 6 AoE on HP loss. Underrated. Power gets out of deck. Decent act 1 AoE bridge
1-cost +2 str. Way worse in Spire 2 — strength irrelevant when playing infinite attacks. Only for act 1
1-cost 5 block + 5 damage. Strike+defend in one card. Prevents chip in hallways. Skip if possible
2-cost deal 5 on block gain. Clad not blocking frequently. No Feel No Pain combos anymore. Bad
1-cost power, copies 3rd attack played. Actively bricks infinite. Almost never want. Complete brick
3-cost rare, 15 damage + enemy loses 10 str this turn. Costs all energy. Should cost 2. Top of C
1-cost 10 damage, double enemy vuln, exhaust. Vuln stacking strategy doesn't work. Desperation pick
0-cost lose 6 HP, +2 energy, draw 3, exhaust. Top of S tier. Insane acceleration. Life loss irrelevant
1-cost next attack plays extra time. No big attacks to double. Not good in Spire 1, not good here
0-cost 17 AoE, needs 3+ cards in exhaust. Condition trivially easy. Great payoff. Bottom of S / top of A
2-cost scales with strikes. Falls off fast. Desperation pick. Strikes get removed at shops anyway
1-cost 6 damage, draw until non-attack. Can redraw entire deck. Infinite enabler. Good in act 1 AND endgame
1-cost 9 damage + draw 1 (draw 2 upgraded). Main infinite enabler at COMMON. Goes infinite with Bloodletting
0-cost transform attacks into Giant Rock (16 damage). Carries act 1 single-handedly. Useless after
2-cost gain 1 energy/turn. Gets you through early game. Hard-capped, can't generate exponential energy
0-cost +3 block per attack this turn. Top of B. Turns Pommels into block cards. Great act 1, falls off
1-cost +5 damage each play. Much improved from Spire 1 — better numbers, smaller decks. Kills act 1 bosses
1-cost +1 str on HP loss. Strength just not needed when going infinite. Needs upgrade. Bottom of C
1-cost exhaust all non-attacks, gain block per card. Main enabler. Enables Pillage, clears statuses. Bottom of S
1-cost 7 damage + 2 temp str. Strength this turn doesn't pay off, Clad not spamming 0-costs
1-cost 8 block + draw 1. Act 1 card. No sundial infinite needed, turns off Pillage. Much worse than Spire 1
0-cost 6 damage, draw 1 on HP loss. Infinite with 2 Spites + Bloodletting. At worst it's Anger. Pick on sight
2-cost power, random attack plays end of turn. Cheats energy. Decent act 1. Bottom of B
1-cost exhaust hand, draw equal cards, exhaust. Fiend Fire + Dark Embrace on ONE card. Upgrades to 0-cost. Insane
3-cost 12 AoE, -1 cost per attack played. Often free. Best AoE at uncommon. Great act 1 AND endgame. Bottom of S
1-cost +4 plating (decays 1/turn now). Much worse than metallicize. Blocks ~10 total. Top of C
1-cost 3 damage x3 random. Strength way worse, can't target, AoE fights more prevalent. Complete crap
Multiplayer-focused rare power. Block generation, niche
1-cost 7 block + 1 vuln. Extends Bash vuln, lets you block and not rush. Combos with Vicious for draw
2-cost hits per HP lost this combat. If you lost HP enough for this, you already won via Bloodletting loops
1-cost rare, 4x2 damage + exhaust random attack from hand + add its damage. Solid scaling, exhausts cards
1-cost 4 AoE + 1 vuln all. Much better with Vicious in pool (draws per vuln). Combos with Stomp. Act 1 pick
1-cost apply 2 vuln. Better vuln options exist. Bottom of C
1-cost 7 block + random exhaust. Demands upgrade for targeted exhaust. More pickable than Spire 1 due to exhaust value
1-cost 5 damage x2. Middling, strength payoff worse in this game
2-cost rare, double first block gain each turn. Can kill a boss but completely useless in endgame
2-cost 12 damage, next attack costs 0. Cheats energy, plays Bash free next turn. Decent hemo alternative
2-cost 13 damage + weak + vuln. Weak less important when not blocking. Kills act 1 boss upgraded. Clunky
1-cost power, draw 1 per vuln applied (draw 2 upgraded). Rushdown but better. Base deck draws cards. Enables infinite
X-cost AoE. Mediocre without strength/upgrade. Needs upgrade which is harder to get. Outclassed by Stomp
